data.title: Nasdaq reports rise in short interest across listed securities as of September 15 settlement date
data.description:
Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) said on Wednesday that short interest across its listed securities increased as of the mid-September settlement date from August 29.

Short interest in 5,014 securities totaled 17,146,159,646 shares at the September 15 settlement date, compared to 4,988 issues and 16,958,657,192 shares at the end of August.

This is 2.01 days average daily volume, compared with an average of 1.97 days for the prior reporting period.

The open short interest positions reported for each Nasdaq security reflect the total number of shares sold short by all broker/dealers regardless of their exchange affiliations.

Short interest in 3,341 Nasdaq Global Market securities totaled 14,035,983,054 shares as of September 15.

Meanwhile, short interest in 1,673 securities on The Nasdaq Capital Market totaled 3,110,176,592 shares at the end of the September 15 settlement date.

data.title: KalVista to offer $110M in convertible notes; shares down 10%
data.description:
• KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) plans to offer $110M in convertible senior notes due 2031 via private placement.
  • An additional $15M in notes may be issued if initial purchasers exercise their option within 13 days.
  • The unsecured notes will mature on October 1, 2031, with semi-annual interest payments.
  • Notes are convertible under certain conditions and may be settled in stock, cash, or both, at KalVista’s discretion.
  • Redemption by KalVista is not allowed before Oct 1, 2028; partial redemption may occur after that if stock meets certain price conditions.
  • Holders can require repurchase at 100% of principal plus interest upon a "fundamental change."
  • Net proceeds will support working capital, EKTERLY commercialization, and potential future acquisitions, though no acquisitions are currently planned.
  • KALV shares down 10% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Worthington Steel Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77 beats by $0.05, revenue of $872.9M beats by $136.9M

data.title: KB Home GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.10, revenue of $1.62B beats by $30M
data.description:
• KB Home press release (NYSE:KBH): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $1.62B (-7.4% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

  • The company is providing the following guidance for its 2025 full year:

  • Housing revenues in the range of $6.10 billion to $6.20 billion.
  • Average selling price of approximately $483,000.
  • Homebuilding operating income as a percentage of revenues of approximately 8.9%, assuming no inventory-related charges.
  • Housing gross profit margin in the range of 19.2% to 19.3%, assuming no inventory-related charges.
  • Selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of housing revenues in the range of 10.2% to 10.3%.
  • Effective tax rate of approximately 23%.
  • Ending community count of approximately 260.
  • Shares -0.45% AH.

data.title: Lithium Americas surges 95% on report of possible U.S. government stake but 'clock is ticking'
data.description:
Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC) shares nearly doubled Wednesday, settling +95.7%, following reports that the Trump administration is seeking an equity stake in the company as it renegotiates terms of a $2.3 billion U.S. government loan.

The company acknowledged it is in talks with the Department of Energy and General Motors, its joint venture partner in the Thacker Pass lithium project in Nevada, regarding the conditions of its loan but did not comment on the reports of a potential government stake.

The news also lifted other lithium miners, including Standard Lithium (SLI) +20.1%, Lithium Argentina (LAR) +7.5%, Sigma Lithium (SGML) +7.4%, Albemarle (ALB) +1.8%, SQM (SQM) +1.1%.

The clock is ticking for Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC), which is experiencing a cash crunch and needs the government funds to keep operating, according to The Wall Street Journal, which added that if the company is unable to reach a deal with the Trump administration, it may be forced to mothball the Thacker Pass project.

U.S. officials reportedly are concerned about the viability of the project and wary that funds would be invested in a volatile industry dominated by Chinese players.

A government stake "would lend credence to project completion and expansion to multiple phases with perhaps enhanced economics," TD Cowen analyst David Deckelbaum said in a note, and media reports are "provocative considering outsized returns in names like Intel and MP, but tangibly for LAC, the upside is less clear given committed offtake constraints, though certainly improves confidence in long-term financing."

To the extent the U.S. government believes equity stakes have a significantly lower political cost than tax increases, "mark-to-market equity stakes can offset deficit financing, higher deficit spending can support corporate profits, interest rates can trend lower to support equity valuations, and equity markets can view equity stakes as a leading indicator" of favorable return on invested capital, Jefferies analysts wrote.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:LIT), (ILIT), (BATT)

data.title: Steelcase Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.09, revenue of $897.1M beats by $23.5M

data.title: Transocean announces public offering of shares
data.description:
• Transocean (NYSE:RIG) intends to offer and sell 100M shares, par value $0.10, in an underwritten public offering.
  • Transocean intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for the repayment or redemption of indebtedness, including the repayment or redemption of a portion of the $655 million aggregate principal amount of the 8.00% senior notes due February 2027 issued by Transocean International, a wholly owned subsidiary of Transocean.
  • RIG -7.7% after hours to $3.36.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. stocks fall as tech sector struggles, rate-cut uncertainties rattle markets
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, marking a second day of declines, as struggling U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

The S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended -0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) finished -0.4%.

This “disappointing day” for the markets “was in spite of some positive news in the housing market,” Daniel Jones, investing group leader of Crude Value Insights, told Seeking Alpha.

“My suspicion is that the market is digesting [Federal Reserve rate cuts] news and recognizing that, while rate cuts are positive in the long run, the economic situation that we find ourselves in is precarious,” Jones added.

“With jobs data continuing to come in weak, consumer confidence near multi-year lows, and other data looking disappointing even as inflation looks to be on the rise because of tariffs, I suspect more weakness will occur at some point. And that's simply because I anticipate the U.S. economy entering into a recession in the coming months,” he said.

On the tech front, OpenAI is considering renting AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) -0.8% to reduce costs associated with the new $100B data center partnership between the two companies.

Micron Technology (MU) edged lower -2.8%, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand. Adobe (ADBE) -2.4% and Oracle (ORCL) -1.6% are also pushing the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) lower.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.61%. A $70B, 5-Year Note Auction slightly tailed.

On the economy, new home sales surged 20.5% MoM in August to 800k. Consensus was for 649k.

Business uncertainty about revenue growth rose to 3.83% in September, up from 3.77% in August, according to an Atlanta Fed survey. Also, CFOs recorded greater optimism in Q3, the Richmond Fed survey showed.

Traders will now await jobless claims data on Thursday and PCE inflation data on Friday.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

data.title: TNF Pharmaceuticals announces name change to Q/C Technologies
data.description:
• TNF Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:TNFA) announced that it has changed its corporate name to Q/C Technologies.
  • The stock will begin trading under the new ticker symbol “QCLS” on Nasdaq, effective as of the market open on Thursday, September 25, 2025
  • The new name Q/C Technologies represents the company's recent strategic pivot into quantum-class computing, it said.

data.title: Costco FQ4 earnings: What to expect
data.description:
Costco’s (NASDAQ:COST) fourth-quarter revenue is expected to jump 8% as the company prepares to report earnings on September 24, after market close.

The company is projected to report a revenue of $86.06 billion, while the consensus EPS estimate is $5.80.  

Seeking Alpha analyst Luca Socci noted that the company’s solid comparable sales and membership growth remain in focus ahead of the earnings.

Costco reported that yearly comparable sales were up 5.9% YoY, with North America up 6.2%, showing a counter trend among peers who reported stronger international growth but saw weakness in North America.

Socci sees Costco’s annual revenue coming up to just above $275B.

“I expect Costco's net income margin to be between 2.9% and 3%, which means its net income will be close to, if not slightly above, $8B. Since Costco doesn't do buybacks, the share count is flat at 443M. Therefore, the projected EPS is $18.01. The result is a PE of 52,” he added.

Despite the encouraging financial metrics, the analyst rated COST as a Hold over valuation concerns. The stock has lost nearly 21% on a YTD basis, while in the past year it has declined a little over 10%.

Over the last 1 year, COST has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 25% of the time. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 4 upward revisions and 11 downward moves. Revenue estimates have seen 9 upward revisions and 5 downward moves.
